title: FiRe 2026: AI Meets the University
snippet: With each passing day, AI tools are changing the nature of academia. Join us for a discussion of the complex opportunities AI presents to universities, researchers, and students; and of the ongoing endeavor to better perfect human knowledge and understanding. Speakers: Pradeep Khosla, Chancellor, UCSD.
match_reason: social (video) — Pradeep Khosla is the featured speaker in this YouTube video discussing the impact of AI on higher education.

title: Statement from UC Academic Senate on admissions review
snippet: The Academic Senate is not rescinding its commitment to a comprehensive review of standardized testing in admissions. Recognizing the significance of this issue, the Academic Senate is revising its timeline while ensuring the forthcoming review is thorough, evidence-based and informed by faculty expertise.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Ahmet Palazoglu is the University of California Academic Senate Chair who issued this official statement regarding the review of admissions policies, which are being reevaluated in part due to the widespread adoption of AI.

title: Preparing for heightened AI cybersecurity risks
snippet: A new report from the National Academies of Sciences, Engineering, and Medicine suggests artificial intelligence will fundamentally reshape the cybersecurity landscape... Giovanni Vigna, PhD, director of the AI Institute for Agent-based Cyber Threat Intelligence and Operation at the University of California, Santa Barbara, noted that 'cybersecurity will need to improve rapidly to meet this challen
match_reason: websearch (press_quote) — Giovanni Vigna is quoted directly in this article regarding his co-authored National Academies report on AI and cybersecurity.

title: Assembly Committee Adopts Resolution Urging Intersegmental Dialogue on AI in Higher Education
snippet: The Assembly Higher Education Committee adopted SCR 82, a resolution urging California's public higher education segments—the UC, CSU, and California Community Colleges—to convene a joint workgroup to coordinate on best practices for AI in instruction, student privacy, and campus services.
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This legislative development directly impacts UC systemwide AI policy and mandates intersegmental collaboration on academic integrity, teaching, and privacy.

title: At the CSU, We See Access to Technology as an Equity Issue
snippet: In an op-ed, California State University leadership discusses their systemwide AI strategy to prevent an 'AI digital divide.' Highlighting a survey of 94,000 respondents, they argue that limiting access deepens inequity and makes it harder to teach the responsible and ethical use of AI.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — This item addresses AI access, equity, and systemwide strategy at a peer California public university system, which is highly relevant to the committee's charge.

title: CSU's AI Survey Reveals Mixed Reception to ChatGPT Across Campuses
snippet: The California State University (CSU) systemwide AI initiative, which deployed ChatGPT Edu to 470,000 students and 63,000 faculty, faces critical decisions as its $17 million OpenAI contract nears its July 2026 expiration. A systemwide survey of 94,000 respondents revealed that while 84% use ChatGPT, faculty remain deeply divided and a student-led petition with over 3,300 signatures has called for
match_reason: websearch (news_article) — This item directly addresses systemwide AI procurement, student/faculty sentiment, and the financial and ethical trade-offs of enterprise AI contracts in California public higher education.

title: Time for Class 2026 Highlights the Intersection of AI-Integrated Assessment and Student Engagement
snippet: Tyton Partners released its 'Time for Class 2026' report, subtitled 'That AI Tipping Point,' marking the first year where over half of students, faculty, and administrators report using AI weekly. The study, surveying over 3,300 higher education stakeholders, reveals that 47% of faculty are actively modifying assessment designs because generative AI has exposed the limitations of traditional gradi
match_reason: websearch (publication) — This major national report provides critical data on how generative AI is forcing a redesign of academic assessments and student engagement strategies across higher education.

title: The Challenges of Adopting Generative Artificial Intelligence in Higher Education
snippet: Presented at the DigiTech 2026 Congress, this paper outlines a comprehensive framework for generative AI governance in higher education. It synthesizes recent global data, including the HEPI survey showing 94% of students use AI for assessed work, and advocates for a transition away from simple productivity gains toward deep pedagogical integration, assessment redesign, and robust institutional go
match_reason: websearch (publication) — This paper provides a structured governance framework covering academic integrity, equitable access, and policy development that aligns with the committee's mandate.

title: CSU's AI Strategy: Preventing a Digital Divide Through Equitable Access
snippet: In an op-ed detailing the California State University's systemwide AI strategy, leaders argue that access to technology is a fundamental equity issue. By providing free, structured access to AI tools, the CSU aims to prevent an 'AI digital divide' where low-income and underrepresented students are left to navigate and pay for advanced technology on their own.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — This piece directly addresses the committee's focus on AI access, equity, and the digital divide for underrepresented students within California's public university systems.

title: CSU Launches 'AI in July 2026' Educational and Showcase Series
snippet: The California State University launched 'AI in July 2026,' a month-long series of online sessions, panel discussions, and campus showcases highlighting AI initiatives across its 22 universities. The series focuses on ethical AI integration, workforce preparation, and practical workshops to establish baseline AI literacy for university staff and faculty.
match_reason: websearch (other) — This systemwide initiative demonstrates how a peer California institution is operationalizing AI literacy, professional development, and ethical training at scale.

title: WARP-RM: A Warp-Augmented Relative Progress Reward Model for Data Curation
snippet: Scaling imitation learning requires large datasets, yet human teleoperation inevitably produces mixed-quality demonstrations containing hesitations and recoveries. Prior frame-level progress reward models supervise on absolute temporal progress proxies that suffer from label noise, or require costly human annotations to define subtask boundaries. title: T-Rex: Tactile-Reactive Dexterous Manipulation
snippet: The ability to react dynamically to tactile signals has long been considered crucial to agile human-level dexterity. Yet contemporary learning-based Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models for robotic manipulation generally either overlook the tactile modality or are limited to encoders with static cues, due in part to the scarcity of diverse training data and standardized evaluation, architectural co

title: “Gender, Power, and AI”: How gender bias, male-centered mythologies, and resource extraction reproduce and amplify inequalities
snippet: Noble, author of the groundbreaking book Algorithms of Oppression, turned to Black feminism and transnational feminism frameworks to help contextualize the larger costs of these choices, both in terms of extraction and waste and in the effects of the technologies themselves on users.
match_reason: websearch (talk) — Safiya Umoja Noble was a featured panelist in this Stanford Clayman Institute event discussing the intersection of gender, power, and artificial intelligence.

title: Webinar: Implications of AI for Cybersecurity
snippet: On June 24, the National Academies will release Implications of Recent Advancements in Artificial Intelligence for Cybersecurity, a rapid expert consultation that examines how recent advances in generative AI and agentic systems are reshaping the cybersecurity landscape. Director Giovanni Vigna contributed to the material supporting this webinar.
match_reason: websearch (position_statement) — Giovanni Vigna is the director of the ACTION Institute and contributed directly to the material supporting this National Academies publication and webinar.

title: CSU Chancellor's Office: Access to AI is an Equity Issue
snippet: In an op-ed, CSU leadership outlines its systemwide AI strategy, emphasizing that access to AI tools is a critical equity issue. A survey of 94,000 CSU students and faculty revealed that over half of students and 60% of faculty regularly use AI, highlighting the need for guided, ethical access.
match_reason: websearch (op_ed) — It provides a direct peer-system perspective on preventing an 'AI digital divide' and managing equitable student access to generative AI.
